pages.full_text: [Printed brochure page — no handwritten diary text. Printed headings and body text from a museum guide: FRENCH DOLLS, ART DÉCO GLASS, NANCY SCHOOL GLASS, PERFUME BOTTLES, with accompanying printed photographs of dolls and glass objects.]
pages.notes: This page is a printed museum brochure page (Museum of Art Nouveau and Art Deco, Casa de Lis, Salamanca) glued into the diary. No handwritten diary text is present on this side. Printed text includes sections on French Dolls (mentioning firms Jumeau, Bru, Gaultier, Thuillier, Schmitt and Steiner, and the Société Française de bébés et jouets), Art Déco Glass (mentioning René Lalique, 1860-1945, and Ernest Sabino), Nancy School Glass (mentioning Emile Gallé, 1846-1904, the Daum brothers, Majorelle, Legra, Paul Nicolas), and Perfume Bottles (mentioning René Lalique, Coty, Houbigant, Worth, Guerlain, Gallé, Daum brothers, Baccarat, Saint Louis).

pages.full_text:
We drove to the end of the Roman Bridge at Salamanca again + walked across + up into the Museum of Art Nouveau + Art Deco in the Casa de Lis. The perfume bottles are lovely the dolls. We spent quite a time discussing them.
We had lunch on the pavement + drove to a le Clerk super market.
pages.notes: Handwritten diary text is written across the middle of a printed museum brochure page (same Salamanca museum guide as the left page). The printed sections visible are PORCELAIN (mentioning Limoges, Sèvres, Bavaria, Türingen, Milton, Bohemia/Zsolnay, Campodimonte, Lenci, Benlliure, Serrá, Spain) and CHRISELEPHANTINES (mentioning Demetre Chiparus, Ferdinand Preiss, Otto Poertzel, Ronald Paris, Joseph Lorenzl, Claire Colinet). A '5' appears to be printed (not handwritten) in the top right corner of the brochure — not a diary page number. The phrase 'the perfume bottles are lovely the dolls' may have a word omitted between 'lovely' and 'the' — possibly 'as are' or similar; transcribed verbatim. 'le Clerk' likely refers to Leclerc supermarket (French supermarket chain), consistent with travel in France/Spain. The Roman Bridge reference and Casa de Lis museum confirm a visit to Salamanca, Spain. Volume B covers 2003-2019; page 150 of 373 suggests approximately 2011-2012 era, consistent with European road trip.
